title: Kong API Gateway Plugin Crash on Request Transformation
symptoms: Intermittent 500 Internal Server Error from Kong gateway (8% of requests)
Kong error logs showing Lua runtime nil reference error in transformation plugin
Errors correlating with requests containing Unicode characters in headers
Plugin execution metrics showing crashes on specific request patterns
Non-ASCII header values triggering the plugin failure
root_cause: Custom Lua request transformation plugin did not handle multi-byte UTF-8 character encoding in HTTP header values, causing nil reference crash when processing Unicode characters
resolution: Deployed patched version of the transformation plugin with proper UTF-8 handling, enabled Unicode-safe string operations in Lua, and added input validation for header encoding
preventive_actions: Implement UTF-8 input handling testing for all custom Kong plugins
Add plugin crash monitoring with automatic fallback to bypass mode
Create internationalization test suite for API gateway plugins
Implement Lua error handling best practices in plugin development guidelines
Add canary testing with diverse character sets for plugin deployments

timeline.event: First 500 errors appear from Kong gateway
Error pattern identified as intermittent (8% rate)
Lua plugin crash error identified in Kong logs
Plugin temporarily set to bypass mode for affected routes
Patched plugin version developed with UTF-8 handling

ocr_entities: Error: attempt to index nil value (header_value)
Trigger: UTF-8 multi-byte chars in headers
embedding_summary: Kong API Gateway custom Lua plugin crash when processing Unicode characters in HTTP headers. 8% of requests failing with 500 errors for 25 minutes. International users disproportionately affected. Resolved by patching plugin with proper UTF-8 character handling.
